In Memoriam

Researchers in this directory who are no longer with us

The Top 100 and the regional pages list living researchers only, so the directory stays accurate for anyone using it to make contact. This page remembers the 8 researchers from the ranked pool who have passed away, plus a separate listing of foundational figures whose careers predate the digital publication record our pipeline reads from.

Foundational figures

The pipeline behind this site ranks researchers whose work appears on arXiv or in OpenAlex, which mostly means careers active from the mid-1990s onward. The names below predate that coverage. Their work is the bedrock on which modern research is built. They are listed by last name; each contribution is noted at the bottom of the page.

NameInstitutionCountryYears
Lucien SzpiroCity University of New YorkUS1941-2020

Foundational figures: contributions

  • Lucien Szpiro (1941-2020): The Szpiro conjecture on elliptic curve conductors and discriminants is essentially equivalent to the abc conjecture; a central figure in arithmetic geometry

From the ranked pool

NameInstitutionCountryYears
Alan BakerUniversity of CambridgeGBd. 2018
Jerzy BrowkinInstitute of Mathematics, Polish Academy of SciencesPLd. 2015
Harold DavenportUniversity of CambridgeGBd. 1969
Eugene EhrhartUniversity of StrasbourgFRd. 2000
Paul ErdősHungarian Academy of SciencesHUd. 1996
Ming-Chit LiuUniversity of Hong KongHKd. 2023
Alfred J. van der PoortenMacquarie UniversityAU1942-2010
Yuan WangChinese Academy of SciencesCNd. 2021

Maintained by Steve Hubbard (admin@wwiabc.org). Data sources: arXiv, OpenAlex, zbMATH Open, Mathematics Genealogy Project.

Last built July 2026.
